title: @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload and Resource Budgets · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds lib-storage multipart adoption contract, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of lib-storage multipart adoption contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin @aws-sdk/lib-storage 3.1087.0 with its peer S3 client line and wrap Upload behind characterized queueSize, partSize, leavePartsOnError, progress, abort, cleanup, and result contracts.
Inventory v2 ManagedUpload and hand-written multipart behavior, map queue, part, progress, abort, cleanup, and result differences, then migrate to v3 Upload behind contract tests.
Choose PutObjectCommand or lib-storage Upload from body size and replayability, and validate Node Readable, Web stream, Blob, progress-event, checksum, and completion ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Lib Storage Version Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8347 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload workers, part buffers, abort ownership, and cleanup policy; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Lib Storage Version Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for pin @aws-sdk/lib-storage 3.1087.0 with its peer s3 client line and wrap upload behind characterized queuesize, partsize, leavepartsonerror, progress, abort, cleanup, and result contracts. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Lib Storage Version Contract, keep Lib Storage Version Contract connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.
